title: Bulenox Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees

meta_description: Full Bulenox pricing breakdown for 2026: plans from $95/mo, 100% profit retention on first $10K, hidden fees, resets, and who it's actually worth it for.
excerpt: Complete Bulenox pricing guide for 2026 — every account tier, monthly cost, hidden fee, and the 100% profit retention structure explained clearly.
content:
Bulenox Pricing Overview 2026

Bulenox is a futures-focused proprietary trading firm that has built a reputation around its trader-friendly profit structure and flexible evaluation timeline. Before you sign up, you need to understand exactly what you're paying — and when those costs stop. This guide breaks down every Bulenox plan, monthly fee, and cost you'll encounter in 2026, so you can make an informed decision based on your trading style and timeline.

The short version: Bulenox accounts start at around $95 per month for smaller account sizes and scale up to $330+ per month for larger ones. The standout feature is 100% profit retention on your first $10,000 earned — an industry-leading structure that sets it apart from most competitors charging 80/20 splits from day one. For the full picture, including platform experience and payout history, read our Bulenox review.

Bulenox Account Plans and Monthly Costs

Bulenox structures its pricing around account size tiers. Each tier carries a different monthly subscription fee, profit target, and maximum drawdown allowance. Critically, you pay the monthly fee throughout the evaluation phase and continue paying until you reach the "Master" (funded) phase. There is no one-time purchase option.

Account SizeMonthly FeeProfit TargetMax DrawdownMax Contracts
$25,000$95/mo$1,500$1,5004
$50,000$115/mo$3,000$2,50010
$100,000$165/mo$6,000$3,00014
$150,000$230/mo$9,000$4,50020
$250,000$330/mo$15,000$6,50030

Pricing and parameters are subject to change. Always verify current rates on the Bulenox website before purchasing.

The most popular tier is the $50,000 account at $115/month, which balances achievable profit targets against reasonable drawdown limits. It's also the benchmark used in most third-party Bulenox reviews, including ours.

The 100% Profit Retention Structure Explained

This is Bulenox's headline feature, and it's genuinely competitive — with one important caveat worth understanding before you commit.

  • Once you pass the evaluation and reach the funded Master phase, you keep 100% of your first $10,000 in profits
  • After that $10,000 threshold, the profit split moves to approximately 90% in your favor
  • No profit share is taken during the evaluation phase — you're simply trading to hit the target

To put this in perspective: most prop firms offer 80/20 or 85/15 splits from day one, and some take a cut as high as 30% even at funded status. Bulenox's 100% structure on the first $10K means a profitable trader can fully recoup evaluation costs before the split ever kicks in. A trader who pays $115/month for three months — $345 total — covers that expense in roughly the first few hundred dollars of funded profit.

The structure is designed to attract serious traders and reward those who reach funded status quickly. It's less attractive if your evaluation drags on, which leads directly to the next section.

Hidden Fees and Real Costs to Know Before Buying

Prop firm marketing focuses on the best-case scenario. Here's what Bulenox actually costs when you account for the full picture:

Recurring Monthly Fees Until Master Phase

This is the most significant cost variable. Bulenox has no time limit on evaluations — which sounds like a benefit, and it is — but fees keep accumulating regardless of progress. A trader who takes six months to pass the $50,000 evaluation pays $690 before receiving a single funded dollar. At nine months, that's $1,035. Slow evaluations are expensive evaluations, and no time limit does not mean no cost pressure.

Reset Fees

If you breach your drawdown limit during evaluation, you can reset the account rather than purchasing a new subscription. Reset fees are typically discounted versus the full monthly rate, but they still represent an additional out-of-pocket expense. Budget for at least one reset if you're earlier in your trading development.

No Refund on Evaluation Fees

Bulenox does not refund monthly subscription payments upon passing or failing. Every dollar paid during evaluation is the cost of access, regardless of outcome. This is standard practice in the industry but worth confirming before you start.

Platform and Data Feed Costs

Bulenox is compatible with popular futures trading platforms, but platform licensing and exchange data fees are typically separate expenses. If you're using NinjaTrader, Sierra Chart, or similar, factor those costs independently. Depending on your setup, this can add $30–$100/month to your true all-in cost.

Withdrawal Minimums and Processing

Check the current minimum withdrawal threshold before signing up. Bulenox has historically maintained reasonable withdrawal terms, but confirming the minimum and processing timeline protects you from surprises after you've earned your first funded profits.

Who Is Bulenox Best For in 2026?

With a 4.1/5 rating, Bulenox isn't trying to be everything to everyone. Its pricing and rules are built around a specific type of trader. Here's who gets the most value:

Futures Day Traders

Bulenox is exclusively futures. If you trade ES, NQ, CL, GC, or similar CME products and you're already profitable on a simulated account, the structure is built for you. Forex spot, stocks, options, and CFDs are not supported — full stop. This isn't a drawback if futures is your market; it's simply a constraint to be aware of upfront.

Algorithmic and Expert Advisor Traders

Bulenox explicitly permits algorithmic trading and Expert Advisors, which many competing firms restrict or ban entirely. If your edge is systematic — running scripts, bots, or coded strategies — Bulenox is one of the few prop firms where you can deploy your system without worrying about rule violations triggering account termination.

Methodical Traders Who Don't Want Time Pressure

The no-time-limit evaluation is a genuine competitive advantage for disciplined traders who refuse to force trades to meet arbitrary deadlines. If your strategy is lower-frequency but consistently profitable, this structure rewards patience. The trade-off is cumulative monthly fees — so patience must also mean profitability within a reasonable window.

Not the Right Fit For

  • Traders who want access to forex, equities, or options
  • Traders who need a refundable entry fee or free trial period
  • Traders who expect the evaluation phase to take many months (monthly costs compound significantly)
  • Traders without an existing edge — no structure makes an unprofitable strategy profitable

Bulenox vs. Competitors: Quick Comparison 2026

How does Bulenox pricing stack up against the alternatives? The table below captures the key cost and structure differences across four firms serving overlapping audiences:

FirmStarting PriceProfit SplitAsset ClassesAlgo TradingEvaluation Time Limit
Bulenox$95/mo100% first $10K, then ~90%Futures onlyYesNone
Apex Trader Funding$167/mo100% first $25K, then 90%Futures onlyYesNone
AquaFunded$39/moUp to 90%Forex, indices, cryptoRestricted30 days
Blue Guardian$89/moUp to 85%Forex, metals, indicesYesNone

A few observations worth drawing out from this data:

  • Bulenox is cheaper than Apex Trader Funding for equivalent account sizes, but Apex's first-$25K retention threshold is higher, which may matter for traders with larger profit targets
  • AquaFunded's lower entry price comes with a 30-day time limit and restricted algo use — two constraints Bulenox specifically removes
  • Blue Guardian offers more asset class diversity but tops out at 85% split versus Bulenox's 100% first-$10K structure

For more detailed head-to-head breakdowns, see our Apex Trader Funding vs. Bulenox comparison, the AquaFunded vs. Bulenox comparison, and the Blue Guardian vs. Bulenox comparison.

Is Bulenox Worth the Cost? Final Recommendation

Bulenox earns its 4.1/5 rating for good reason. The combination of no time limits, full algorithmic trading support, and 100% profit retention on the first $10,000 creates a cost structure that genuinely favors the trader over the firm — which is not as common in the prop trading industry as marketing materials suggest.

The math is straightforward when you run through the scenarios:

  • Pass evaluation in 1–3 months: Total cost $95–$345 at the $50K tier. With 100% profit retention on your first $10K funded, you're in positive territory quickly. Excellent value.
  • Pass evaluation in 4–6 months: Total cost $380–$690. Still justifiable if your trading expectancy is strong, since the 100% retention window more than covers your fees in the funded phase.
  • Evaluation takes 6+ months: Costs compound to $690 and beyond. At this point, the more honest question is whether you've validated your edge sufficiently before committing to a subscription. Consider whether more sim time is the right answer first.

For futures traders who already have a proven edge — and especially those running algorithmic strategies — Bulenox is one of the most competitive prop firm offers available in 2026. The futures-only limitation is a non-issue if that's your market. If you need forex, equities, or options exposure, the right firm is a different conversation.

Want the full breakdown before making your decision? Our Bulenox review covers platform experience, payout history, real trader feedback, and a final score across every category that matters.
